mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and funding stress, so equity multiples expand and the S&P 500 posts positive forward 20-day returns when the policy rate is in a sustained downtrend.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, so equity prices re-rate lower and the S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE trigger events are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the triggered-sample mean return is not lower than the unconditional mean at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST marks broadening real-demand strength in consumption and housing, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST improvement does not outperform single-series effects, or if 60-day forward returns after joint expansions are not positive in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in INDPRO relative to demand indicators reflects stronger industrial activity and better corporate revenue prospects, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days when industrial production is accelerating.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 0.5% over 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if industrial production acceleration is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power versus INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high but stable FEDFUNDS rate combined with contained CPIAUCSL inflation indicates restrictive real policy is not tightening further, which removes uncertainty and supports modest positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-rate / stable-inflation condition is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the signal disappears once CPI is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in GDPC1 growth signals weakening aggregate demand and lower forward earnings, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 60 trading days when quarterly real GDP slows materially.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with slowing GDPC1 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal is not distinguishable from noise after revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth stalls while UNRATE rises, labor income momentum weakens and consumer spending expectations deteriorate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth turns negative over 3 months and UNRATE rises over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll slowdown and unemployment rise do not forecast lower 20-day returns, or if triggered events produce positive returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led slowdown, declining HOUST precedes weaker construction activity, softer broad demand, and lower equity returns, so the S&P 500 should fall over the next 20 trading days after housing starts break down.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start breakdowns are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of events, or if the effect vanishes outside housing downturn regimes.
